title: done
text: done Exit from the ProcScript module without changing $status . done Return Values None. The done statement does not affect $status . Use Allowed in all component types. Description The statement done immediately exits from the ProcScript module. If you want to return a value, use the return statement rather than done . The following example shows how to use the done statement: trigger quit if ($formmod = 0) done else message "Data modified!! Use STORE before QUIT." return (-1) endif end; quit Copy Related Links break Unconditionally exit a loop initiated by for, forentity, forlist, repeat, or while. end Marks the end of a ProcScript module, operation, or trigger. exit Exits the current component instance and returns to the previous or specified instance. return Exit from the ProcScript module, optionally returning a value to $status.
